SEARCH
Package de.dhbw.memory.controller

Class GameService

java.lang.Object
de.dhbw.memory.controller.GameService
@Service @UIScope public class GameService extends Object
Spring service that orchestrates one Memory game per browser tab.

@UIScoped is a Vaadin scope that ties the bean lifetime to a single UI instance (= one browser tab). Each tab therefore gets its own game state, independent of other open tabs.

Typical call sequence from the view layer:

  1. startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) — build board and create a new Game.
  2. flip(int, com.vaadin.flow.component.UI, java.lang.Runnable) on every card click — returns a FlipResult immediately.
  3. On FlipResult.NO_MATCH the service automatically schedules the flip-back after 1500L ms and notifies the view via the afterReset callback (run inside UI.access(com.vaadin.flow.server.Command)).
Author:
Markus Wenninger

    FlipResult
    flip(int position, com.vaadin.flow.component.UI ui, Runnable afterReset)
    Flips the card at position and returns the outcome.
    long
    getElapsedSeconds()
    Returns the number of whole seconds that have passed since startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) was called.
    Game
    getGame()
    Returns the current Game, or null if startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) has not been called yet.
    Theme
    getTheme()
    Returns the Theme chosen for the current game, or null before startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) has been called.
    boolean

    Returns true while the flip-back delay after a mismatch is in progress.

    Shuts down the background scheduler when the Vaadin UI (browser tab) is closed.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameService

      public GameService()

  • Method Details

    • startGame

      public void startGame(List<String> playerNames, int gridSize, Theme theme)
      Creates a new game with the given configuration and replaces any previous game.
      Parameters:
      playerNames - one or two player names (non-empty strings)
      gridSize - board edge length — must be 4 or 6
      theme - card theme that determines motif names

    • flip

      public FlipResult flip(int position, com.vaadin.flow.component.UI ui, Runnable afterReset)
      Flips the card at position and returns the outcome.

      On FlipResult.NO_MATCH this method schedules a background task that waits 1500L ms, calls Game.resetMismatch(), then runs afterReset inside UI.access(com.vaadin.flow.server.Command) so the view can re-render without blocking the UI thread (requirement #FANF09).

      While the flip-back delay is in progress, every subsequent call returns FlipResult.INVALID to prevent the player from clicking more cards.

      Parameters:
      position - 0-based grid position of the clicked card
      ui - the Vaadin UI of the current browser tab; required for the async push-back on NO_MATCH (may be null when no mismatch can occur, e.g. in unit tests)
      afterReset - callback executed inside ui.access() after the flip-back delay; the view should re-render the board here
      Returns:
      the result of this flip attempt

    • getGame

      public Game getGame()
      Returns the current Game, or null if startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) has not been called yet.

    • getTheme

      public Theme getTheme()
      Returns the Theme chosen for the current game, or null before startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) has been called.

    • getElapsedSeconds

      public long getElapsedSeconds()
      Returns the number of whole seconds that have passed since startGame(java.util.List<java.lang.String>, int, de.dhbw.memory.model.Theme) was called. Used by the status bar timer.

    • isWaitingForFlipBack

      public boolean isWaitingForFlipBack()
      Returns true while the flip-back delay after a mismatch is in progress. The view should disable card buttons in this state.

    • shutdown

      @PreDestroy public void shutdown()
      Shuts down the background scheduler when the Vaadin UI (browser tab) is closed. @PreDestroy is called by Spring when the scoped bean is destroyed, preventing thread leaks.
